title: Substack vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs.
h1: Substack vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Substack and Constant Contact serve different purposes in the email marketing landscape. Substack is built for writers looking to monetize newsletters directly through subscriber payments, while Constant Contact targets small businesses and nonprofits needing comprehensive marketing automation. Understanding their strengths will help you choose the right platform for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_b: Email marketing for small businesses and nonprofits

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, recommendations
comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social media ads, e-commerce integration

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to monetize your audience directly through subscriptions
You need unlimited subscribers on a free plan with no upfront costs
You want built-in podcast hosting and audience discovery features
choose_b_if: You run a small business or nonprofit and need comprehensive email marketing tools
You require SMS marketing, social media ads, and e-commerce integration
You want event management and advanced list-building capabilities

faq.q: Is Substack better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your use case. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing newsletters, while Constant Contact is superior for businesses needing multi-channel marketing automation. Choose based on whether you prioritize direct subscriber revenue or broader business marketing tools.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper if you're starting out, as it offers a completely free tier with unlimited subscribers. Constant Contact requires a minimum $12/month investment. However, if your Substack grows substantially, Substack's 10% revenue cut may eventually cost more than Constant Contact's flat fee.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your email list from Constant Contact and import it to Substack, though the process requires manual steps. Keep in mind that Substack is designed for newsletter publishing rather than traditional business email marketing, so some Constant Contact features won't have direct equivalents on Substack.
